snapshot:
Your total cholesterol and triglycerides sit above functional range across this draw, while vitamin D and ferritin are below their functional floors. The lipid picture is the biggest single lever; D and ferritin both have meaningful food-first paths.

Fasting glucose is in functional range, hemoglobin and WBC look fine. The plan below focuses on food moves that hit cholesterol, triglycerides, vitamin D, and iron stores at once.

insights.title: Total cholesterol above functional range
insights.detail: 244 mg/dL sits well above the functional ceiling of 200. Soluble fiber, plant sterols, and reducing saturated fat are the highest-leverage food moves.

insights.title: Triglycerides elevated
insights.detail: 165 mg/dL is above the functional ceiling of 80. Strongly diet-driven; carbohydrate quality is the lever.

insights.title: Vitamin D in low-normal range
insights.detail: 32 ng/mL is at the bottom of lab normal and well below the functional 50-80. Sun exposure plus dietary D will move it.

insights.title: Iron stores at functional floor
insights.detail: 65 ng/mL is below the male functional minimum of 70. Heme-iron foods are the lever.

eatList.why: Brings omega-3s for the lipid profile; also a real vitamin D source.

eatList.portion: 1/2 cup oats OR 1 cup cooked beans OR 1 tbsp psyllium
eatList.why: Pulls cholesterol out via bile-acid binding — the most direct dietary lever.
Folate + magnesium + plant nitrates support cardiovascular and methylation pathways.
Most absorbable form of iron; addresses ferritin directly.

avoidList.why: Pro-inflammatory and tied to higher cardiovascular risk markers.
avoidList.swap: Olive oil, avocado oil, or ghee

avoidList.food: Refined grains (white bread, white pasta, pastries)
avoidList.why: Drive triglyceride elevation through rapid glucose spikes.
avoidList.swap: Whole oats, intact grains, or legumes

lifestyle.title: 10-minute walk after dinner
lifestyle.why: Improves post-meal glucose and triglyceride disposal.
lifestyle.how: Right after eating, around the block or up and down the stairs.

lifestyle.title: 10 minutes morning sun on skin
lifestyle.why: Endogenous vitamin D synthesis is the most efficient route.
lifestyle.how: Within an hour of waking; arms + face, no sunscreen for that window.

supplements.title: Vitamin D3 + K2
supplements.why: Vitamin D at 32 ng/mL needs replenishment beyond what food provides; K2 directs calcium correctly.
supplements.how: 2000 IU D3 + 100 mcg K2 (MK-7) with the largest fat-containing meal.
supplements.caution: Retest D in 12 weeks. Stop if you start anticoagulants — discuss with clinician.

habitStack.intro: Five small actions that compound across the week.

habits.title: 1/2 cup oats with breakfast
habits.cue: First thing, on the stove or microwave
habits.why: Soluble fiber pulls cholesterol out via bile-acid binding.

habits.title: Vitamin D3 + K2 with biggest meal
habits.cue: Lunch or dinner, whichever has fat
habits.why: Brings vitamin D into functional range.

habits.title: 16 oz water on rising
habits.why: Hydration improves daily energy and supports kidney clearance.

retest.reason: Lipid response to food + walking shows up at 12 weeks.
D3 supplementation moves measurably in 12 weeks.
